subject: Dangerous pothole on Mannheim near St. Charles
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing because there is a massive pothole on Mannheim Road just south of the St. Charles Road intersection. It's been there at least three weeks and I watched a minivan blow out a tire on it this morning. My front left wheel hit it Wednesday and my alignment is definitely off now. Can the Village get crews out to fix this? It's a state road I know but surely there's something you can do. This is a real safety hazard.
address_hint: Mannheim Rd at St. Charles Rd

subject: Dangerous pothole on Mannheim near St. Charles
body_clean: Thank you for reaching out and I'm sorry to hear about your alignment — that's genuinely frustrating. Mannheim Road is a state-maintained route under IDOT District 1 jurisdiction, which means the Village of Bellwood does not have authority to repave or patch it directly. I've already forwarded your report to our IDOT District 1 contact with the specific location noted. You can also submit directly at idot.illinois.gov or call 847-705-4000. We'll follow up with IDOT to push for a timely repair.
address_hint: Mannheim Rd at St. Charles Rd

subject: Dangerous pothole on Mannheim near St. Charles
body_clean: Thank you for the quick response and for contacting IDOT on my behalf. I didn't realize Mannheim wasn't a Village road — that's good to know. I'll file a report on their website too so there's a paper trail from multiple residents. Appreciate you following up rather than just passing the buck.
address_hint: Mannheim Rd at St. Charles Rd

subject: Signal timing at Mannheim and Washington is terrible
body_clean: I commute through the Mannheim/Washington intersection every single morning and the light cycle makes no sense. The southbound green on Mannheim lasts maybe 25 seconds but there are 15-20 cars backed up. Washington gets a long green with almost no cross traffic. Who sets these timers? Can the Village adjust them? I've been late to work twice this month because of the backup.

subject: Signal timing at Mannheim and Washington is terrible
body_clean: Terrence, I hear you — that backup is real and I've seen it myself. Unfortunately signal timing on Mannheim Road falls under IDOT, not the Village. The signals at state-route intersections are programmed and maintained by IDOT District 1 out of Schaumburg. I'm going to send a formal request to our IDOT contact asking them to review the cycle at Mannheim and Washington specifically. Your description of the 25-second southbound green is exactly the kind of detail they need. I'll let you know what they come back with.

subject: Left turn from Mannheim onto Bohland Ave is a death trap
body_clean: Mayor — the left turn from northbound Mannheim onto Bohland Avenue is genuinely dangerous. There's no protected left arrow, and cars come over the hill doing 50+ mph. I saw a near miss there last Tuesday around 7:40 a.m., a silver sedan nearly got T-boned. I have three kids who cross at that corner on their way to school. This needs a protected turn signal or at least better signage. Please do something.

subject: Left turn from Mannheim onto Bohland Ave is a death trap
body_clean: Ms. Ndiaye, the Mayor asked me to respond right away. The Mannheim/Bohland intersection is on a state right-of-way controlled by IDOT, so adding a protected left-turn phase requires IDOT approval and engineering review — it's not something the Village can do unilaterally. That said, Mayor Okonkwo is writing to IDOT District 1 requesting a formal safety study of that intersection, including the sight-line issue you described. If you'd like to add weight to the request, you can email IDOT directly at district1.public@illinois.gov. We'll keep you in the loop.

subject: Mannheim Road cracking up badly between Hirsch and Harvard
body_clean: Mayor, the stretch of Mannheim between Hirsch Ave and Harvard Ave is in rough shape. Longitudinal cracks running almost the full block, and there's a bad edge drop-off on the right shoulder where cyclists try to ride. I know it looked like IDOT did a mill-and-fill here maybe 4-5 years ago but whatever they did didn't hold. Just wanted to flag it in case the Village can advocate for a proper resurfacing project in IDOT's next capital cycle.
address_hint: Mannheim Rd between Hirsch Ave and Harvard Ave

subject: Streetlights out on Mannheim near Morris Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I walk home from the Metra station around 10 pm and three streetlights on Mannheim near Morris Avenue have been dark for at least two weeks. It's pitch black in that stretch. I'm a woman walking alone and I'm nervous every single night. Who do I call? Is this ComEd, is this the Village, is this IDOT? Nobody seems to know.

subject: Streetlights out on Mannheim near Morris Ave
body_clean: Ms. Fontenot, your safety matters and I want to give you a clear answer. Streetlights on Mannheim Road are a shared situation: the poles and wiring are generally ComEd-owned on state routes, but IDOT coordinates maintenance requests. I've already notified our IDOT contact about the outage at Mannheim near Morris. Please also report it directly to ComEd at 1-800-334-7661 — their outage line is the fastest path to a repair order. In the meantime, if you'd feel safer, Officer Banks from our community liaison office can note your usual route for extra patrol attention.

subject: Pothole field on Mannheim south of Madison
body_clean: hey mayor just wanted to let you know mannheim south of madison is absolutely destroyed right now. feels like driving on the moon. my car bottomed out twice last week. i know you guys probably can't fix a state road but can you at least yell at idot? thanks
address_hint: Mannheim Rd south of Madison St

subject: Mannheim / 25th Ave signal — pedestrian phase too short
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am 71 years old and I cannot cross Mannheim at 25th Avenue in the time the walk signal allows. I've timed it: I get approximately 14 seconds before the hand starts flashing, and I use a cane. I've nearly been caught in traffic twice. I understand this may be an IDOT signal but I'm asking the Village to intervene and request a longer pedestrian phase. Older residents live on both sides of that intersection.

subject: Mannheim / 25th Ave signal — pedestrian phase too short
body_clean: Ms. Shepherd, thank you for writing and for being so specific — the 14-second count is exactly what I need to make a compelling case. You're correct that signals on Mannheim are IDOT's responsibility, but that doesn't mean Bellwood stays quiet. I'm sending a written request to IDOT District 1 today asking for an ADA-compliant pedestrian phase review at Mannheim and 25th Ave, and I'm cc'ing our state representative's district office to add some pressure. I'll personally follow up with you once I have a response from IDOT.

subject: Faded lane markings on Mannheim — very confusing at night
body_clean: Mayor — the lane markings on Mannheim between Washington Blvd and St. Charles Rd have faded to almost nothing. At night in the rain last Thursday I genuinely couldn't tell which lane I was in. I've seen drivers drifting around because there's no guidance. Repainting lane lines seems like a simple fix but I assume this is IDOT's call. Can the Village push them on it before someone gets hurt?
address_hint: Mannheim Rd between Washington Blvd and St. Charles Rd
